title: Success Criteria, Baselines, and Prompt Evals · Contract Clinic: Success Criteria
summary: Define and test a support-triage prompt before optimizing its wording. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt success criteria increment in a versioned prompt evaluation harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Turn subjective quality goals into observable criteria and representative test cases.
Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Success Criteria
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt success evals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt success evals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Success Criteria contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for turn subjective quality goals into observable criteria and representative test cases.

content.text: Turn subjective quality goals into observable criteria and representative test cases. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt success evals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Success Criteria, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a versioned prompt evaluation harness.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A prompt is good when one impressive example works. This fails the Success Criteria contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Anthropic Prompt Engineering Overview (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls success criteria, empirical evaluation, and the living Claude prompt-engineering workflow.

steps.title: Build Success Criteria evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-success-criteria-r1kctp" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Success Criteria into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-success-criteria-r1kctp
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the Success Criteria model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Success Criteria applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Turn subjective quality goals into observable criteria and representative test cases.
